description_long: This object is a short, straight-bladed knife with a hexagonal cross-section and a pointed tip. The blade appears to be made of metal, showing a darkened, possibly oxidized or stained surface near the tip, suggesting frequent use or exposure to the elements. The handle is integrated with the blade, lacking any clear separation or ergonomic grip, and is colored a worn yellow-green, possibly painted or coated wood or metal. The overall form is utilitarian, with no visible guard or decorative features, and the object is relatively compact and lightweight. The knife's design is simple and functional, likely intended for basic cutting or carving tasks. Its weathered appearance suggests it has seen significant use, possibly in outdoor or workshop environments.
description: A compact, straight-bladed knife with a pointed tip and a worn yellow-green handle, showing signs of heavy use and oxidation.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the knife, highlighting the pointed tip and the yellow-green coloration along the handle.
description_view_1: End-on view showing the hexagonal cross-section and the thickness of the blade and handle.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the straight blade and pointed tip with visible wear.
description_view_3: Reverse end-on view, emphasizing the handle's width and the transition to the blade.
attribute_handle_design: hexagonal, straight, painted, no ergonomic features
